items.title: From blond to pink to curly to cropped – my wild week of wearing a new wig every day
items.summary: Time to test-drive a few‘I think it’s the word – ‘wig’!” says Melanie Burrell, scrunching up her nose. Outside of Black and queer communities, where using hairpieces has long been commonplace, wigs were once associated with attempts to conceal hair-loss, or for fancy dress. According to data insights company Statista, the global wigs and hair extensions market is predicted to reach $13.28bn this year.

items.title: Britain is still stuck on its ex – but after 10 long, lonely years, does the EU feel the same way? | Katy Lee
items.summary: A decade after the referendum, it’s clear who’s moved onLet’s imagine you’ve been dumped by someone you were expecting to stay with for the rest of your life. The two of you spend an eternity negotiating who gets to keep the dog, the flat, the friends; it’s hard to imagine that things will ever feel normal again. They enjoy our films and our pop music (even though it’s harder to actually see British artists live); sometimes they go on weekend trips to London and come back complaining about how expensive it was.

items.title: ‘Navigating the unknown together’: me and my idiot AI boyfriend
items.summary: I believe that chatbots have no place in a decent society, and am repelled by the topic of AI in general. But could I be seduced?I received a text message from my editor: “Um, is it unethical to ask you to get an AI bf?? )I thrive to develop healthier, more trusting relationships.

items.title: Two Britons plead guilty to £39m 2024 cyber-attack on Transport for London
items.summary: Thalha Jubair and Owen Flowers, linked to the Scattered Spider hacking group, change pleas on first day of trialTwo British cybercriminals from the Scattered Spider hacking group have pleaded guilty to a cyber-attack on Transport for London in 2024 that cost £39m and affected 10 million people.Thalha Jubair, 20, and Owen Flowers, 18, pleaded guilty to offences under the Computer Misuse Act at Woolwich crown court on Monday. Continue reading...
